title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
2-Step Security
2-Step Security is an extra layer of authentication that is added to an application. To log in to an application that is protected by 2-Step Security, a user must enter the one-time password from their registered 2-Step Security Token.
2-Step Security Token
A 2-Step Security Token is a device or application that generates a random one-time password that is recognized by applications that are protected by 2-Step Security.
AML
Anti-Money Laundering.
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT)
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is a Debit or Credit Transaction. Debit Transactions are known as Pre-authorized Debits (PADs). Credit Transactions are known as Direct Deposits.
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Re-presentment
An AFT Re-presentment is an AFT debit transaction that has been returned for the return reason code 901 â NSF, or code 908 â Funds Not Cleared, and is re-submitted on a one-time only basis.
Branch Number
A Branch Number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number, in conjunction with the 3-digit Institution Number, makes up the Transit Number.
Business Day
A Business Day is a weekday including Monday to Friday except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday.
Business PAD
A PAD drawn on the account of a Payor for the payment of goods or services related to a business or commercial activity of the Payor including, but not limited to, payments between franchisees and franchisers, distributors and suppliers, and dealers and manufacturers.
Cash Management PAD
A Cash Management PAD is a Pre-authorized Debit drawn on the account of a Payor for the purpose of transferring, consolidating, or repositioning funds between the Payorâs account at one financial institution to their account at another financial institution, for the same business or closely affiliated businesses (e.g., transfers between a parent company and its subsidiary).
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications, and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application.
Credit Reporting Agency
A Credit Reporting Agency, also known as a consumer reporting agency, provides Credit Reports as follows:
for gain or profit (e.g., Equifax Canada provides a Credit Report to a financial institution that purchases one), or
on a routine, non-profit basis (e.g., a financial institution provides a Credit Report to Equifax Canada to update an individualâs Credit Information and maintain the integrity of the credit reporting system).
Credit Transaction
A Credit Transaction is a credit Payment Item exchanged in an AFT File as defined in CPA Standard 005. Also known as a Direct Deposit.
Customer ID
The Customer ID is the 10-digit number assigned by Central 1 that identifies the Originator of an AFT file. Also known as the Originator ID.
Debit Transaction
A Debit Transaction is a Pre-authorized Debit within the meaning of Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, exchanged in an AFT file as defined in CPA Standard 005.
Direct Clearer
A Direct Clearer is a member of the CPA that meets the requirements of the association and settles for clearing items through a settlement account at the Bank of Canada.
Direct Deposit
A Direct Deposit is an AFT Credit Transaction such as a payroll deposit.
Due Date
The Due Date is the date on which the Payor is to be debited or the date on which the Payee is to receive payment.
Error Correction
An Error Correction is an AFT Transaction used to correct an error made by the Originator or the Originating Direct Clearer. An Error Correction corrects a transaction(s) posted to the recipientâs account(s) in error. Error Corrections apply to both transactions and AFT files.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
File Creation Number
A File Creation Number is a consecutive number automatically assigned to an AFT file during preparation for transmission. Central 1 uses the File Creation Number for verification purposes because files must be received in sequential order.
File Error Correction
A File Error Correction is an AFT Transaction that is sent to the Processing Direct Clearers to correct transactions that were posted to the recipientsâ accounts in error. A File Error Correction transaction is generated for each transaction in the original AFT file. A File Error Correction is sometimes referred to as a Mass Error Correction or a File Reversal.
File Recall
A File Recall is when an Originator requests the return of an AFT file that has not yet been processed at Central 1. See Recall.
Financial Institutions File
The Financial Institutions File (FIF) is an electronic directory of branches of deposit-taking institutions in Canada and is maintained by Payments Canada. It includes information to facilitate the processing and exchange of payment items between deposit-taking institutions in Canada.
FINTRAC
The Financial Transaction and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada.
Funds Transfer PAD
A Funds Transfer PAD is when funds are drawn on the account of a Payor at one institution and transferred to an account of the same individual at another financial institution (Payor and Payee are the same). A Funds Transfer PAD can include, but is not limited to, registered savings plans, mutual funds, segregated funds, annuities, deposit accounts, cash accounts, and investment accounts.
Hard Token
A Hard Token is a physical 2-Step Security Token.
Institution Number
The Institution Number is the 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that can be used on paper and electronic payment items to identify a financial institution.
Lead Time
Lead Time is a reference to the number of days before the Due Date that Central 1 must receive an AFT file. The Lead Time is generally two Business Days, but it may be shorter in some cases based on Serviceability Code â0.â
Mass Error Correction
See File Error Correction.
Member Payee
A Member Payee is a financial institution that is also a Payee.
Notice of Change (NOC)
A Notice of Change (NOC) is a non-financial transaction created by the receiving financial institution. It informs the payment Originator of changes to the payment routing information.
Offsetting Transaction
The Offsetting Transaction is the Debit or Credit Transaction posted to the Originatorâs account that equals the total value of the individual debit or Credit Transactions within an outgoing AFT File. The Offsetting Transaction is also a transaction that offsets an AFT item returned through the Online Return System (ORS) or the Batch Return Service if the software does not create the offsetting entry. An Offsetting Transaction can also be referred to as an Offsetting Entry.
ORS
The Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of AFT Transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Originator
An Originator is the financial institution or business member that creates an AFT file.
Originator ID
An Originator ID is the 10-digit number assigned by Central 1 that identifies the Originator of the AFT file.
Payee
The Payee is the entity for which the Credit Transaction is intended.
PaymentStream
AFT
PaymentStream
AFT allows users to create AFT Transactions and upload AFT files.
Payor
The Payor is the entity for which the Debit Transaction is intended.
PCMLTFA
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
.
PCMLTF Regulations
The regulations passed under
PCMLTFA
, including:
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Suspicious Transaction Reporting Regulations
Cross-border Currency and Monetary Instruments Reporting Regulations
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Administrative Monetary Penalties Regulations
Personal PAD
A Personal PAD is a PAD drawn on the account of a Payor for payments such as, but not limited to, charitable donations, non-Member investment contributions, mortgage installments, utility bills, insurance premiums, membership fees, property taxes, credit card billings, lines of credit, loans and payment for other consumer goods or services but not a Business PAD, a Cash Management PAD, or a Funds Transfer PAD.
Pre-authorized Debit (PAD)
A Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) is an AFT transaction issued by a Payee or Member Payee that is drawn on an account of a Payor (e.g., mortgage payment, strata payment). See Debit Transaction.
Pre-authorized Payment (PAP)
See AFT Transaction.
Pre-notification
Pre-notification is the required Written Notice from the Payee or Member Payee to the Payor, indicating the amount and date of a Pre-authorized Debit (PAD). Pre-notification is required prior to the date of the actual PAD. It is also required to make a change to the amount or date of a PAD.
Recall
A Recall is a request to return an AFT transaction that has not been posted to the receiverâs account. A Recall applies to individual Credit Transactions and to AFT files. Recalled AFT files can contain both credits and debits. Individual debits can only be recalled if Central 1 has not sent the transaction to the data centre of the receiving financial institution. See File Recall.
Receiving Financial Institution
The Receiving Financial Institution is the financial institution that receives AFT Transactions from another financial institution.
Reject
A Reject is a transaction in the AFT file that cannot be processed by Central 1, the other data centre, or the Receiving Financial Institution.
Restricted Entity
A Restricted Entity is an entity involved in one or more of the following:
Money services businesses, including currency exchanges
Money transmitters and casas de cambios, including payment services providers
Casinos and gambling establishments
Internet gambling/gaming-associated businesses and websites
Marijuana providers and/or distributors
Virtual currency exchangers and administrators
Serviceability Code
The Serviceability Code is a code on the Financial Institutions File (FIF) that represents the number of Business Days required to credit a Payeeâs account with an AFT Credit Transaction on the date those funds are to be made available.
Set Interval PAD
A Set Interval PAD is a PAD that occurs at specified, set, or predictable periods or times; or upon the occurrence of such criteria and/or event(s) as may be set out in a Payorâs PAD Agreement.
Settlement Date
The Settlement Date is the date on which Central 1 posts a transaction to the financial institution. This is usually the Due Date.
Soft Token
A Soft Token is a 2-Step Security token that is installed on the userâs mobile device as a mobile app.
Sporadic PAD
A Sporadic PAD is a PAD that occurs occasionally, irregularly, intermittently, infrequently, periodically, and not at a set interval or one time. The Payee is required to obtain authorization for each Sporadic PAD. The Payor cannot waive this authorization.
Stale Dated
An AFT transaction that is back-dated more than 173 calendar days (for debits) or 30 calendar days (for credits). These transactions are rejected.
Transit Number
The Transit Number consists of the Branch Number (five digits) plus the Institution Number (three digits). Each financial institution has a unique Transit Number (e.g., 80912010).
Transaction Recall
See Recall.
Transmission File
A Transmission File is a file of AFT transactions that is uploaded to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
US AFT Transaction
A US dollar AFT Transaction is a transaction between two Canadian financial institutions in US dollars.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
Written Notice
Any form of representing or reproducing words in visible form, including an electronic document, provided the electronic document is under the control of the intended recipient, the information contained in the electronic document is substantially in the same form as a paper copy, and the information contained in the document is accessible if requested.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
PAD Agreements
Question 1
We have business members who want to use their own version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement. Is that okay?
Answer
Yes. However, if the financial institution allows business member Originators to use a version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement that differs from the Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696, you must ensure that it complies with CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits. The mandatory and supplementary elements of a Payorâs PAD Agreement are provided in CPA Rule H1.
Question 2
CPA Rule H1 refers to the Letter of Undertaking. What is that?
Answer
The Letter of Undertaking is the agreement between a financial institution and its client (the Payee Originator) that originates PADs. Central 1âs version of this document is the Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904 (for Personal PADs and Business PADs) and the Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007 (for Cash Management PADs). You must enter into one of these agreements when a business member is enrolled as a PAD Originator. See Chapter 5, Enrolling in the AFT Service, for more information.
Question 3
Is a Letter of Undertaking required for Funds Transfer PADs?
Answer
No. Funds Transfer PADs are transactions that credit a memberâs account at the financial institution and debit their account at another financial institution. The financial institution is the Originator. However, the member must sign a Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696, or equivalent form. See Chapter 5, Enrolling in the AFT Service, for more information.
Question 4
Is a Payorâs PAD Agreement required for Cash Management PADs?
Answer
No. Completion of the Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007, is adequate.
PAD Types and PAD Timing
Question 5
What are the PAD types and why does it matter which type an Originator uses?
Answer
The PAD type provides a description of the PAD and is encoded in the AFT transaction details. The four types of PADs that Originators can use are:
Personal PADs:
Used by consumers to pay companies or other organizations for goods or services.
Funds Transfer PADs:
Used to move money between accounts held by the same person at different CPA member financial institutions. Common examples include contributions to investment accounts, mutual funds, and registered savings plans.
Business PADs:
Used to pay for goods or services related to a business or commercial activity of the Payor (e.g., payments between franchisees and franchisors, distributors and suppliers, or dealers and manufacturers).
Cash Management PADs:
Used to consolidate or reposition funds between accounts held by the same business or closely affiliated businesses at different financial institutions (e.g., a parent company can use a Cash Management PAD to draw funds from an account of its subsidiary).
The main reason that it is important to use the correct PAD type is that recourse time frames, recourse eligibility, and valid reasons for reimbursement claims differ by PAD type. Another reason is that other processes such as authorization procedures and pre-notification requirements are also affected by PAD type.
Question 6
How can I tell if I should set up a Cash Management PAD or a Funds Transfer PAD? They seem to serve a similar purpose.
Answer
Funds Transfer PADs are for personal use. They allow an individual member to debit their account at another financial institution and credit their account at the financial institution. Cash Management PADs are similar but are reserved for business use. Specifically, funds are debited from the business member or its subsidiaryâs account at another financial institution and credited to its account at the financial institution.
Question 7
What is the difference between Sporadic and Set Interval PADs and why is it important to make the distinction?
Answer
A Sporadic PAD is one that occurs irregularly. A Set Interval PAD occurs at specified, set, or predictable times, or upon the occurrence of criteria or events that are described in the Payorâs PAD Agreement. CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, states that the timing of a PAD (Sporadic or Set Interval) must be provided in the Payorâs PAD Agreement. It is important to determine the timing of a PAD because if the PAD is sporadic, the Payee Originator must obtain authorization from the Payor for every PAD. This authorization cannot be waived. See CPA Rule H1 for more information on Sporadic and Set Interval PADs.
AFT Limits
Question 8
How do we increase our business memberâs AFT file limit?
Answer
In
PaymentStream
AFT, limits are set on the Originator, not the user. There is a transaction limit, a settlement day limit, and a monthly limit. Financial Institution administrators have the authority to change the limits for all originators.
Exception Items
Question 9
What is the difference between an AFT Recall and an AFT Error Correction?
Answer
A Recall is used to return a transaction or a file that has not yet been posted to the recipientâs account. An Error Correction is used to reverse a transaction or a file that has been posted to the recipientâs account(s) in error. A Recall is not an AFT Transaction. It is simply a request to return an unposted transaction. Recalls are processed on a best-efforts basis.
Question 10
Can I issue a Recall or Error Correction for individual AFT transactions and AFT files?
Answer
Yes. You can request a Recall or an Error Correction on individual transactions or on an entire AFT file (e.g., the file was sent in error). See the AFT Rejects, Recalls, Error Corrections, and Other Exception Items Part for more information, including Recall and Error Correction deadlines.
Question 11
Is it true that a dishonoured PAD can be re-presented?
Answer
Yes. A PAD can be re-presented by AFT Originators who are enrolled in the AFT Re-presentment service. However, the PAD can only be re-presented (re-entered into the Clearing) if it was returned for the reasons Non-Sufficient Funds (code 901) or Funds Not Cleared (code 908). If re-presented, the amount of the PAD must be the same as the original transaction.
AFT Re-presentment Service
Question 12
What is the maximum number of days for AFT Re-presentment?
Answer
Central 1âs AFT Re-presentment service has a limitation of eight business days following the return of the original AFT debit, although Payments Canada allows for Re-presentment to occur within 30 days.
Question 13
Is there a common number of days or best practice number of days for Re-presentment?
Answer
No. The number of business days for Re-presentment can be unique to each AFT Originator in what they feel is adequate time for their payors to have proper funds in their account (from zero to eight days). As a suggestion, it could be based on when one-time debits may have been re-generated in the past.
Question 14
Do we need to make changes in our banking system to accommodate the AFT re-presented items?
Answer
No. There are no development preparations required from the credit union or banking system to use the AFT Re-presentment service.
Question 15
Can the AFT Re-presentment process be tested for an AFT Originator?
Answer
No. There is no test environment available for AFT Re-presentment.
Question 16
On the PaymentStream AFT Service Application, Form 5329, which account number do we enter for âSettlement Account Information for Offset CR for AFT Re-presentment Onlyâ?
Answer
Leave the account number field blank for âSettlement Account Information for Offset CR for AFT Re-presentment Onlyâ
if
the AFT Offset CR is to be directed to the same AFT Originatorâs settlement account number that was entered in the previous field for âSettlement Account Information.â
Some financial institutions may be using a specific account for the AFT Offset credits. To accommodate, Central 1 has provided an option to have the Offset CR go to a different account. If this applies to your financial institution, enter the usual AFT Originatorâs settlement account number in the âSettlement Account Informationâ field, and enter the account number specific to AFT Offset credits in the âSettlement Account Information for the Offset CRâ field.
Question 17
When opting for the AFT Re-presentment service, can an AFT Originator pre-determine which of their customerâs AFT pre-authorized debit records can be set for AFT Re-presentment?
Answer
No. The AFT Re-presentment service cannot isolate which AFT debit records are subject to Re-presentment. When an AFT Originator applies for Re-presentment service, all their AFT debits returned as 901 and/or 908 are eligible for Re-presentment.
AFT Re-presentment Workflow
Question 18
Is there anything to prevent a previously returned pre-authorized debit from being re-presented a second time?
Answer
Yes. CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions, stipulates those AFT debits returned for reason codes 901 and/or 908 cannot be re-presented a second time. Central 1 has built this regulation into the AFT Re-presentment service.
Question 19
What does our business member see on their end in
PaymentStream
AFT?
Answer
The AFT Re-presentment service is not a feature of
PaymentStream
AFT so your business member Originators will not see any reference to AFT Re-presentment within
PaymentStream
AFT. However, if your business members retrieve Central 1âs AFT Settlement Reports via the MERG file, they will see re-presented transactions listed by âAFT Re-presentment Originator IDsâ on the reports.
Question 20
Will it appear to our business member that the transaction has been re-presented?
Answer
This may be unique to each financial institution as each may have their own internal process to handle returned AFT debits that are subsequently submitted as a re-presented item. If your business member retrieves Central 1âs AFT Settlement Reports via the MERG file, they will see re-presented transactions listed by âAFT Re-presentment Originator IDsâ on the reports.
Question 21
How will the AFT Re-presentment payment flow?
Answer
Based on the number of days for AFT Re-presentment, Central 1âs AFT Re-presentment service generates an AFT re-presented debit for distribution to the destination financial institution. Additionally, an offsetting AFT credit is created for posting to the AFT Originatorâs account at the financial institution.
Question 22
How are the re-presented debits identified on the reports?
Answer
AFT re-presented debits are listed on separate AFT reports (ICDN, ICRM, and ICTI) under the specific AFT Re-presentment Originator ID.
Question 23
Does a re-presented PAD come through one of our AFT clearing files just like any other PAD?
Answer
A re-presented debit does not appear in the AFT clearing files. The re-presented PAD goes out to the other financial institution. It is the offsetting CR for the re-presented AFT debit that appears in the AFT clearing file. The offsetting credit also appears on the corresponding Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD) report on the day the AFT Re-presentment occurs. The report will have the AFT Representment OID listed under Orig Bank User ID along with the indicator âREPRESENT.â
Question 24
How do re-presented debits appear in the memberâs settlement account so that they can reconcile between the initial item and the re-presented items?
Answer
This may be unique to how a financial institution decides to manage reconciliation of AFT debits that have been re-presented. Some may choose to post the offsetting CR for any re-presented debit to an alternate holding account. See Question 16.
Fees
Question 25
How are fees for AFT Originating and for AFT Re-presentment distinguished for business members?
Answer
Central 1 does not provide any fee reporting directly to business members. All fee reporting is directed to your financial institution. There is separate reporting for the original AFT Originator ID and AFT Re-presentment Originator ID.
Question 26
How will my financial institution identify which fees are for the original AFT Originator ID and which are for the AFT Re-presentment Originator ID?
Answer
Fees are listed separately on the AFT Monthly Fee (AFTM/UFTM) report by Originator IDs â for the original AFT Originator ID and for the AFT Re-presentment Originator ID. Additionally, separate Monthly Customer AFT Billing (ICCB/UCCB) reports are created for the original AFT Originator ID and for the AFT Re-presentment Originator ID.
END
